enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct grammar or vocabulary item.

questions.texte: Choose the correct relative pronoun: “The woman ______ lives next door is a volunteer at the local clinic.”
Rewrite using the passive voice: “The government launched a new anti-erosion campaign.”
questions.reponse: A new anti-erosion campaign was launched by the government.

questions.texte: Fill in the blank with a modal of speculation: “She isn’t sure yet, but she ______ emigrate to Canada next year.”
Turn into reported speech: “The Mayor said, ‘We will sort out our garbage collection system.’”
questions.reponse: The Mayor announced that they would sort out their garbage collection system.

questions.texte:
Match the vocabulary word to its definition:
1. vaccine    2. curriculum    3. perpetrator    4. topsoil
a) person who commits a crime   b) course of study   c) upper layer of earth   d) preventive medical substance

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions below.

In many rural communities in Cameroon, young people are organizing themselves to clean up water sources, build small waste collection points, and teach hygiene practices. These volunteers receive no salary but gain valuable skills, self-confidence, and respect from their elders. Local authorities are now partnering with them to expand these initiatives.

questions.texte: List two benefits of volunteering mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Gaining valuable skills, self-confidence, and respect from elders (any two).

questions.texte: Explain why local authorities are partnering with the youth volunteers.
questions.reponse: To expand the initiatives (e.g., clean water, waste collection, hygiene teaching).

questions.texte: Write a sentence using “in order to” expressing the purpose of volunteering.
questions.reponse: Example: “They volunteer in order to improve their community.”

enonce.texte:
You bought a smartphone online, but it arrived with a cracked screen and the battery does not last more than two hours. Write a formal email of complaint to the customer service department.

Include: subject line, polite greeting, description of the problem, what you want (refund or replacement), and a closing.

questions.reponse:
Example: 
Subject: Complaint about faulty smartphone – Order #12345

Dear Customer Service,

I am writing to complain about a smartphone I purchased from your website on 10 March. The phone arrived yesterday with a cracked screen, and the battery lasts less than two hours.

I would like a full refund or a replacement. Please let me know how to proceed.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

enonce.texte:
Your school wants to reduce cases of malaria among students. Using Type 1 conditionals, write three health tips for the school magazine. One example is given.

Example: If you sleep under an insecticide-treated net, you reduce the risk of mosquito bites.

questions.reponse:
Example answers:
1. If you eliminate standing water around your home, mosquitoes cannot breed.
2. If you take antimalarial prophylaxis as prescribed, you are less likely to get malaria.
3. If you notice a fever, you get tested immediately.

enonce.texte:
As Senior Prefect, you must address the rise of violence in your school. Write a short speech (100–150 words) including:

  • a cause (e.g., lack of conflict resolution skills)
  • a consequence (e.g., injuries, fear)
  • a solution (e.g., peer mediation club)
  • linking words: because, therefore, however, although

questions.reponse:
Example: 
Dear fellow students,

Violence has increased in our school because many of us lack conflict resolution skills. Therefore, students get injured and others feel afraid to come to class. However, this situation can change. Although it will take effort, I propose creating a peer mediation club to help solve disagreements peacefully. Let us work together to make our school safe for everyone.
